citRespParty.rpOrgName: RI Department of Administration, Division of Statewide Planning

idCitation.otherCitDet: RIGIS, 2022. Land Use and Land Cover (2022). Rhode Island Geographic Information System (RIGIS) Data Distribution System, URL: https://www.rigis.org, Environmental Data Center, University of Rhode Island, Kingston, Rhode Island (last date accessed: 1 April 2022)

dataIdInfo.idAbs:
A statewide, seamless, vector-formatted geospatial dataset depicting 2020 land use and land cover ground conditions. The product was developed by comparing high resolution 2020 and 2011 leaf-off aerial orthoimagery and employing both automated and manual processes to detect, delineate and photointerpret changes since 2011.

The project area encompasses the State of Rhode Island and also extends 1/2 mile into the neighboring states of Connecticut and Massachusetts, or to the limits of the source orthoimagery. The minimum mapping unit for this dataset is 0.5 acre.

The classification scheme is based on the same RI-modified Anderson Level III scheme used in previous classifications (1988, 1995, 2003/2004, and 2011) with the addition of two new classes (148) Ground-mounted Solar Energy Systems and (149) Wind Energy Systems. If data are used for change detection using the 2003/2004 edition be aware that marinas were coded from other transportation and developed recreation to commercial in the 2020 data to more accurately fit the classification system. The RI classification is based upon Anderson Level III coding described in the United States Geological Survey Publication: "A Land Use And Land Cover Classification System for Use With Remote Sensor Data, Geological Survey Professional Paper 964" Available Online at: http://landcover.usgs.gov/pdf/anderson.pdf

Please consider the source, spatial accuracy, attribute accuracy, and scale of these data before incorporating them into your project.

These data were derived from both automated and manual photointerpretation processes and should be used for planning purposes only. The wetland areas contained in this dataset do not include all wetlands previously identified in other RIGIS land use and land cover datasets or in other separate GIS wetland datasets and interpretation of wetland areas should lean toward the side of caution. Wetland areas previously classified as forested wetlands are shown as forested areas in this dataset. Statistical comparisons with RIGIS land use and land cover data prior to 2003 should be treated with caution since some differences in the methodologies used to delineate features were employed
dataIdInfo.idPurp: A statewide, seamless, vector-formatted geospatial dataset created under the direction of the RI Department of Administration, RI Division of Statewide Planning depicting 2020 depicting land use and land cover ground conditions. This dataset is intended to be incorporated into the Rhode Island Geographic Information System database for use by local, state, and federal government and made available to the general public under established RIGIS licensing procedures. As with any data consult the metadata for more detailed descriptions.
dataIdInfo.idCredit: Rhode Island Department of Administration, Division of Statewide Planning; Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management; University of Rhode Island Department of Natural Resources Science, Environmental Data Center; United States Geological Survey Commercial Partnership Team, Geospatial Products and Services Contracts, National Map Liaison Network

Consts.useLimit: This dataset is provided 'as is.’ The producer(s) of this dataset, contributors to this dataset, the Rhode Island Geographic Information System (RIGIS) consortium, the State of Rhode Island, and the University of Rhode Island do not make any warranties of any kind for this dataset, and are not liable for any loss or damage however and whenever caused by any use of this dataset. Please acknowledge both RIGIS and the primary producer(s) of this dataset in any derived products. Versions of the RIGIS logo suitable for both printed and web-based products are available at https://www.rigis.org/logos. Additional access and use constraints imposed by the data originator: Please consider the source, spatial accuracy, attribute accuracy, currentness, and scale of these data before incorporating them into your project. These data were derived from automated and manual photointerpretation processes and should be used for preliminary planning purposes only. The wetland areas contained in this dataset do not include all wetlands previously identified in other RIGIS land use and land cover datasets or in other separate GIS wetlands datasets and interpretation of wetland areas should lean toward the side of caution. Wetland areas previously classified as forested wetlands are shown as forested areas in this dataset. Statistical comparisons with RIGIS land use and land cover data prior to 2003 should be treated with caution since significant differences in the methodologies used to delineate features were employed.

LegConsts.useLimit: The University of Rhode Island and the Rhode Island Geographic Information System make no warranty, express or implied, related to all aspects of this geospatial product, including its accuracy, reliability, completeness, currentness, and accessibility

report.measDesc: The data went through multiple levels of QC (by the internal production unit at NV5 Geospatial, and by the State of Rhode Island). Therefore the data are deemed to be fairly accurate. However, attribute accuracy is limited by the fact that decisions on use of land were based primarily on the interpretation of aerial imagery which can be sometimes restrictive in distinguishing between commercial and mutli-family housing, commercial and industrial and mixed use in single structures. Where the project team had additional ancillary data (current parcels and land use/land cover data), better classification was achieved. Furthermore, only actual changes on the ground between 2011 and 2020 were mapped. Errors that existed in 2011 were carried through to 2020 if no visible orthoimagery change occurred. Technicians only mapped land use/land cover change and did not correct errors from the 2011 dataset so that change statistics will more accurately reflect actual changes.
report.evalMethDesc: Validation Process: A random stratified sample was used in the validation process to measure the accuracy of digitized change and Anderson Level III classification. The final data meets or exceeds a mapped accuracy of 90%. A validation report was sent along with the data with the statistical results of the stratified sampling.

QuanResult.quanValType: Value 1 - Overall change detection accuracy; Value 2 - Overall accuracy of change classification; Value 3- Kappa value

report.measDesc:
The land use and land cover data is only included for areas of the state of Rhode Island and extending 0.5 mile into Massachusetts and Connecticut.
Small areas of the dataset outside of the state boundary and within the ½ mile buffer did not undergo change detection through this semi-automated process because of the absence of 2011 imagery for those areas. These data carried forward from previous releases of the land use and land cover series. Specifically there are 8 areas totaling 3.3 square miles outside of the western boundary of the state and 8 areas totaling 2.52 square miles outside of the eastern boundary. The RI Team checked over these areas against both the 2011 and 2020 orthoimagery to see if there were any changes meeting the standards for land use and land cover change and there were none.

dataSource.srcDesc: 2011 RIDEM Multispectral Orthoimagery of Rhode Island

srcCitatn.resTitle: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management, United State Geological Survey
srcCitatn.resAltTitle: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management, United State Geological Survey

srcCitatn.resTitle: NV5 Geospatial powered by Quantum Spatial
srcCitatn.resAltTitle: NV5 Geospatial powered by Quantum Spatial

dataSource.srcDesc: Land Use and Land Cover 2011 Vector

srcCitatn.resTitle: RIGIS, 2015. Rhode Island Land Cover and Land Use 2011; rilc11d. Rhode Island Geographic Information System (RIGIS) Data Distribution System, URL: http://www.rigis.org, Environmental Data Center, University of Rhode Island, Kingston, Rhode Island (last date accessed: 8 April 2015).

dataLineage.statement: To provide a statewide dataset representing 2020 land use and land cover for use by the Rhode Island Department of Administration, Division of Statewide Planning.

prcStep.stepDesc: Task Overview: NV5 Geospatial updated the Rhode Island 2011 land use and land cover data layer through the photointerpretation of 2011 and 2020 orthoimagery with a minimum mapping unit of 1/2 acre, except water bodies which were retained in the data under a 1/2 an acre. Photointerpretation: The following processing steps were taken in order to ensure quality and accuracy of the final product. All existing land use/land cover data were checked for gaps, slivers, and topological errors prior to update process. Centerlines of new additional roads were identified and derived from the updated impervious surfaces data. A 40 foot buffer was applied to identified additional road centerlines and intersected with the existing 2011 land use land cover data as per the project technical documentation in order to remove underlying classifications. Utilizing the updated impervious surfaces data and 2011 and 2020 orthoimagery, technicians then adjusted land use/land cover boundaries where true change was identified utilizing Anderson Level III land use code schema. Post Processing: A field update script was used to identify areas of land use/land cover change where Anderson Level III code values where different between fields LULC_2011 and LULC_2020. The Change column was updated with "Yes" if change occurred. Also during the field update process, the data were dissolved (across all fields except FID), exploded, and repaired. The data was also checked for topological accuracy again to ensure that no errors were generated during manual photointerpretation. Any polygon artifacts inside or next to change that were below the minimum mapping unit were identified and inspected by a senior editor to ensure accuracy.

Small areas of the dataset outside of the state boundary and within the ½ mile buffer did not undergo change detection through this semi-automated process because of the absence of 2011 imagery for those areas. These data carried forward from previous releases of the land use and land cover series. Specifically there are 8 areas totaling 3.3 square miles outside of the western boundary of the state and 8 areas totaling 2.52 square miles outside of the eastern boundary. The RI Team checked over these areas against both the 2011 and 2020 orthoimagery to see if there were any changes meeting the standards for land use and land cover change and there were none.
Data contributed to RIGIS by the NV5 Geospatial. Metadata were reviewed, minor corrections made to formatting and content, RIGIS distribution information updated. Process Date: 20220322

enttyp.enttypl: Rhode Island 2020 Land Use and Land Cover Classification
enttyp.enttypd: Table containing attribute information associated with the dataset.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The state the land use and land cover resides in

attrdomv.udom: Valid State codes are: CT - Connecticut; MA - Massachusetts; RI - Rhode Island
3-digit RI modified Anderson Land Use and Land Cover Classification code

attr.attrdef: Anderson Level III description based on LULC_2020 code

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal measurement units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal measurement units squared.

distorOrdPrc.resFees: • No fee for downloading directly from the RIGIS Data Distribution System located at https://www.rigis.org

onLineSrc.orDesc: Original source dataset as distributed by RIGIS using the RI State Plane feet (NAD83) (EPSG 3438) coordinate system.

Consts.useLimit: Please consider the source, spatial accuracy, attribute accuracy, and scale of these data before incorporating them into your project.
